# Send the request to the website

# Check the response code

# Read the response body

# Extract the sensitive information from the response body

# Write the sensitive information to the file

# Print a success message

else

# Print an error message
echo "Error: $response"

fi

# Clean up the temporary file
rm "$file\_name"

# Exit with a status of 0 to indicate success
exit 0

done

First, we need to check if the webcam is connected and if it is, we will get the device name.

2. Next, we will use the `ffmpeg` command to capture the video from the webcam and save it to a file.

3. We will then use the `img-capture` command to capture a screenshot of the video and save it to a file.

4. Finally, we will use the `mplayer` command to play the video and take a screenshot every 5 seconds.

```
#!/bin/bash

# Check if webcam is connected
if [ -z "$1" ]; then
  echo "Please provide the device name of the webcam"
  exit 1
fi

# Get the device name
DEVICE_NAME=$1

# Capture video from webcam
ffmpeg -f v4l2 -i /dev/video/$DEVICE_NAME -c:v libx264 -preset slow -crf 18 -movflags +faststart output.mp4

# Capture screenshot every 5 seconds
while true; do
  # Capture screenshot
  img=$(img-capture -l 1 -t 500000000 -f %i -o capture.png /dev/video/$DEVICE_NAME)

  # Play video and take screenshot
  mplayer -vo fbdev -framedrop -vo fbdev -f v4l2 /dev/video/$DEVICE_NAME -c:v libx264 -preset slow -crf 18 -movflags +faststart output.mp4 && sleep 5 && capture.png

  # Wait for 5 seconds
  sleep 5
done
```
